steps.description: Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type 'View: Show Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select "View: Show Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" from the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Preview README.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code interface, located in the central editor section, near the top of the tab view.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in the Visual Studio Code interface while the "Overview of the Basic Tab template" file is displayed in the editor.
Type the text 'Microsoft 365 Accounts' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette, which is invoked by the blue highlighted search bar at the top of the window.
Click on the "Accounts: Manage Accounts" option in the dropdown menu under the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" search field in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Type text: 'Sign in to Azure' into the search bar located at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface within Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the "Sign in to Azure" action in the Visual Studio Code interface's account section, interacting with the highlighted dropdown selection.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 authentication prompt to proceed with Azure account authorization.
Click the "Allow" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sign-in dialog box to grant the extension permission to use Microsoft account credentials.
Click the "Use another account" option displayed in the Microsoft account sign-in interface at coordinates (445, 499) to select an alternative login option.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field in the Microsoft Sign-In page to focus the text entry.
Enter ${{env:AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}} into the email or username input field on the Microsoft Sign in page at login.microsoftonline.com in a Chrome browser.
@assertion  ${{env:AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}  is visible
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft Sign-In page, located near the bottom-right corner of the central login form.
Type ${{secret:AZURE_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Password' field on the Microsoft login page for user ${{env:AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Press Enter in the password field on the Microsoft login page to submit credentials for the account ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Click the red close (X) button in the top-right corner of the Google Chrome window to close the Visual Studio Code sign-in confirmation page.
@assertion there's ${{env:AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}} in the "ACCOUNTS" section
